task_description: Build headless WordPress Astro site herbertokolowitz.com
reflection_q1: Should have extracted Language type and SUPPORTED_LOCALES constant from the start — the getStaticPaths sync trap was predictable
reflection_q2: Front-load a shared i18n constants file for any multilingual project; generate shared layout components to avoid index.astro duplication
reflection_q3: Council skill in THINK phase would have been valuable for debating WP URL/API strategy; FirstPrinciples for the i18n architecture

task_description: Archive Astro branch, restore clean static main
reflection_q1: The manual git checkout -- revert step was unnecessary — switching branches auto-restores tracked files. Could have skipped 3 planned commands.
reflection_q2: Test SSH auth earlier (THINK phase as prerequisite) rather than BUILD. Would catch auth issues before any destructive work begins.
reflection_q3: No capabilities were needed here; this was pure git operations. Correct call to skip capability selection for a plan-execution-only task.

task_description: Implement ATOM Obsidian second brain integration
reflection_q1: Could have used prd-utils.ts exports for findLatestPRD and parseFrontmatter — chose self-contained standalone script instead to avoid cross-directory coupling
reflection_q2: Running /simplify mid-EXECUTE rather than post-EXECUTE would catch import and efficiency issues earlier, reducing rework
reflection_q3: prd-utils.ts already had all the PRD parsing I needed — should scan hooks/lib/ for reusable utilities at the start of any tool-writing task

task_description: Add Noller AI Zotero PDFs to NotebookLM
reflection_q1: Could have run test upload earlier to validate auth before full OBSERVE. Worked fine but an early smoke test would be cleaner.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would pre-validate the notebooklm library version compatibility (sources bug with s.type) before trusting the CLI verify step.
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this task — it was purely mechanical upload work. NotebookLM skill was the right sole capability.

task_description: Create Zotero collection with 24 philosophy bibliography items
reflection_q1: Nothing different — clear plan, clean execution, all 8 ISC passed first run
reflection_q2: Pre-check for existing collection name to avoid duplicates on re-run
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this scripting+API task

task_description: Attach Google Drive PDFs to Zotero entries via API
reflection_q1: Should have tested one gws download before building full loop — saved 10min of debugging
reflection_q2: Test a single API/CLI command with one sample item before writing the full batch script
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this scripting task

task_description: Improve TH Ulm Kursvorstellung file with Telos/Zeitplan
reflection_q1: Fetched Google Sheets directly — correct, as Zeitplan had different semester dates than Syllabus.md. Backup created before any edits.
reflection_q2: Could have auto-detected gws flag format from --help first to avoid one retry.
reflection_q3: No additional skills needed — this was pure content/text work, document editing was sufficient.

task_description: Review WLAN map devices improve performance
reflection_q1: Should have skipped per-IP port scanning loop — avahi provided richer data faster
reflection_q2: Smarter approach: avahi first, then targeted probing of unknowns only
reflection_q3: No PAI skills needed for this — pure bash network tooling was the right approach

task_description: Security audit home WLAN 192.168.1.0/24
reflection_q1: No nmap made service identification harder - banner grabs were mostly empty for IoT devices which firewall everything
reflection_q2: Should have checked ZTE F680 CVE database via WebSearch for specific known exploits
reflection_q3: WebAssessment skill would have structured the router assessment better but standard bash was sufficient

task_description: Plan Claude PAI parity on IdeaPad notebook
reflection_q1: Existing replicate-to-laptop.sh was a great starting point - should read it before planning to avoid redundancy
reflection_q2: Could have diffed hook/skill lists programmatically instead of manually comparing ls outputs
reflection_q3: No PAI skills needed - pure inventory + comparison was the right approach

task_description: Execute PAI parity migration to IdeaPad notebook
reflection_q1: Sudo issue with npm required workaround - should check install method before planning update step
reflection_q2: The replicate script + targeted rsync combo worked cleanly - good pattern for future migrations
reflection_q3: No special PAI skills needed - pure bash/rsync execution

task_description: Update SetPieceTakers Notion space with 2026 data
reflection_q1: Should have fetched all page contents before update_content calls to avoid empty-block match failures
reflection_q2: Pre-fetch every target page before update_content — add this as a standard step before any Notion write wave
reflection_q3: Background Agents were listed but not spawned via Task tool — did all work directly which was faster given Notion MCP was available in main context

task_description: Write Monadic Architectures full publication manuscript
reflection_q1: Parallel agent strategy worked excellently for 9000-word academic writing — 4 agents simultaneously writing independent sections saved ~75% wall time. Integration was smooth because each agent had full context in the prompt.
reflection_q2: Should have spawned Section IV in parallel with the other 4 sections rather than sequentially after — it could have been written from its brief without needing actual II/III prose. Would have saved another 5-7 minutes.
reflection_q3: Research skill should have been invoked during OBSERVE to verify Kayadibi arXiv details and Steinhart publication details before committing them to ISC. Both are in manuscript with deferred citation notes now.

task_description: Implement autonomous Finance Tracker GDrive Sheets Claude Vision
reflection_q1: bun run --check executes the script rather than type-checking only; should use bun build --target=bun for compile validation. Should separate dry-run from live execution in CLI tools that modify production data.
reflection_q2: Smarter approach: test each gws API call in isolation (BUILD phase) before writing all files; have a --dry-run flag in index.ts that is the default for first runs
reflection_q3: Research skill for Claude Vision PDF document block format; FirstPrinciples for validating gws API parameters before full implementation

task_description: Fix #ERROR! issues in Drive-Link column L
reflection_q1: Should have pre-validated spreadsheet ID and added dry-run mode before EXECUTE phase
reflection_q2: Smarter design would externalize FILE_IDS/Q_FOLDER_IDS to config file, add retry logic with backoff, output audit trail of formula assignments
reflection_q3: Could have used /batch skill for parallel chunking, but sequential was efficient enough. Research skill not needed for narrow locale-specific task scope.

task_description: Fix bad data in Dateiname column Google Sheets
reflection_q1: Should have pre-validated all patterns with stricter regex before batchUpdate apply; JSON pipe parsing failures were recoverable but inefficient.
reflection_q2: Smarter algorithm would parallelize batchUpdate chunks concurrently, use gws --page-all for large fetches, and always dry-run before applying.
reflection_q3: No special skills invoked; gws CLI directly was sufficient. Standard tier task needed only basic tool invocation and data analysis.

task_description: Scrape PhilPapers metadata via Playwright for 8 URLs
reflection_q1: Should have immediately tried fresh browser instances per URL rather than reusing context — Cloudflare fingerprints browser sessions
reflection_q2: A smarter approach would test one URL first to discover the anti-bot evasion requirements before designing the full scraping loop
reflection_q3: No additional PAI skills needed for this task — direct Python playwright was the right tool

task_description: Add OpenAlex scientific research workflow to Research skill
reflection_q1: Plan was fully spec-d so execution was straight-line. Reading an existing workflow file for formatting conventions was the right first move.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have run a live curl test against OpenAlex API in VERIFY to confirm the endpoint and filter syntax are correct.
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities were needed — the task was pure file creation/editing from a complete spec.

task_description: Projektplan Neue-Ordnung Artikel mit Nusser
reflection_q1: Hätte die OpenAlex-Recherche direkter (via WebFetch selbst) statt via Background Agent durchgeführt werden sollen — der Agent produzierte ein riesiges Output-File, das schwer zu parsen war. Direkte Queries wären effizienter gewesen.
reflection_q2: Ein smarterer Algorithmus hätte die drei Deliverables parallel via separaten Background Agents schreiben lassen, um Zeit zu sparen. Serielle Ausführung war suboptimal für Extended-Level.
reflection_q3: Council-Skill hätte für die Debatte Nusser-Theologie vs. Herbert-Philosophie eingesetzt werden können. Auch WritingSkill für die Email wäre möglich gewesen.

task_description: Research IEA 2025 AI electricity report data
reflection_q1: Parallel search decomposition worked well, covering report identification, US data, and citation format simultaneously
reflection_q2: Could have used WebFetch to pull actual IEA page content for even more precise figure verification
reflection_q3: Could have invoked Research skill formally rather than doing inline WebSearch

task_description: Research 10 academic sources save to Obsidian
reflection_q1: 5 of 10 research agents failed due to usage limits — should have prioritized agent use for hard-to-know sources only
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have divided sources into known vs unknown and only used agents for the latter
reflection_q3: Could have used GeminiResearcher or PerplexityResearcher as backup for failed agents

task_description: Monadic Architecture manuscript backup revise fact-check report
reflection_q1: Should have read all Noller-AI files before launching background agent to avoid redundancy and ensure comparison completeness
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would serialize quality-critical creative tasks (comparison report) and parallelize only research/scanning tasks
reflection_q3: The simplify skill should have been invoked to review the revised manuscript for awkward punctuation substitutions from em dash removal

task_description: Create Section 3 Draft v5 from line-by-line edits
reflection_q1: Should have read the research synthesis file earlier in OBSERVE to better gauge word count gap.
reflection_q2: Smarter algorithm would have flagged the word count gap (1250 vs 2000) as a separate task requiring synthesis integration.
reflection_q3: WriteStory or BeCreative could have been invoked to expand synthesis paragraphs toward 2000 words.

task_description: Write synthesis expansion adding ~750 words to v5
reflection_q1: Expansion paragraphs integrated cleanly without disrupting v5 argument flow.
reflection_q2: Could have used the Council skill to debate where expansion adds most philosophical value.
reflection_q3: BeCreative or WriteStory could have pushed the petites perceptions section to greater philosophical depth.

task_description: implement feedback on v4 create section 3 v5a
reflection_q1: Could have read v5 first before going straight to the outline to understand what had already been done, saving a read of v4.
reflection_q2: A smarter approach would have been to diff v4 vs v5 to see exactly what changed, then layer the remaining feedback on top rather than re-reading both drafts fully.
reflection_q3: BeCreative was selected but not invoked via tool call because the writing itself was done directly. Should have either invoked it or not selected it.

task_description: deploy super mario v2 to valentino games site
reflection_q1: Task was straightforward — SKILL.md had all needed info. Could have gone straight to execute after OBSERVE.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have classified this as NATIVE after reading SKILL.md — it was a 3-step deploy not a complex task.
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed. Bash SSH/SCP was sufficient.

task_description: Deploy super_mario_v2.html to Valentino Games
reflection_q1: Nothing — clean deploy, SKILL.md had exact workflow
reflection_q2: Same approach, possibly skip phases 2-3 for trivial file uploads
reflection_q3: No additional skills needed for a single-file deploy

task_description: Write essay AI responsibility spatial justice Germany
reflection_q1: Address metaphor as rhetorical through-line was the best creative decision and should be applied to similar essay tasks
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have encoded the organizing metaphor in the ISC criteria themselves as a quality requirement
reflection_q3: Council skill would have been valuable to anticipate counterarguments and strengthen the synthesis section
